name: Restaurant, cafe, and food
pitchTone: Warm, hospitality-aware, and practical. Talk like you have already eaten there: assume the owner is on the line or expediting, so be brief and concrete about what a hungry customer needs to see in ten seconds.
valuePropHook: A flat $500 single-page site that puts your menu, hours, address with a map link, and a phone number front and center, so the customer who Googles you at 6pm decides to come in instead of bouncing to the next result.

objections.objection: We just need menu uploads, not a new site.
objections.response: Menus are the core of the page. We put the menu front and center as text so Google can actually read it, plus the hours, the address, and a tap-to-call link; that fixes the most common reason people give up and pick another spot.

objections.objection: We already have a Facebook page.
objections.response: Facebook is great for regulars. The website is for the first-time customer who searches your name on Google, sees a thin business listing, and needs the hours, the address, and a real menu in ten seconds to decide to come in.

objections.objection: DoorDash and Yelp handle our online stuff.
objections.response: Those drive orders, but they do not own the searcher who types your name into Google. The page is the home base those listings point at and the one place that always has accurate hours, the dine-in menu, and your phone number.
Understood. We design the menu section so you can email me an updated PDF or photo and it goes on the page same day, no monthly fee; the structure stays even when the items rotate.

objections.objection: We do not take online orders.
objections.response: Not a problem. The page is built around dine-in and call-ahead: hours, address with a map link, phone number, and a clear menu, with no order button to confuse anyone.

objections.objection: We do not have professional food photos.
objections.response: Most independents do not. We start with whatever you have on your phone or Yelp and leave clean placeholders; you can swap photos in any time without paying again.

objectionMap.skeptical: Fair question. I am Callan from callmemaybe. I was checking public restaurant listings for whether hungry customers can quickly see menu, hours, address, and phone.
objectionMap.ai_disclosure: Yes, I am an AI voice operator for callmemaybe, and this call is automated and recorded. I can explain the restaurant page offer briefly or remove you.
objectionMap.number_source: I used public business listing research for the restaurant, not a private contact list. If you want no more calls, say stop and I will mark it.
objectionMap.pricing: It is a flat $500 for a menu-hours-address page with map and phone front and center. Nothing starts unless you choose to pay the invoice.
objectionMap.send_info: I can send the short version through AgentMail. What email should I use? I will read it back before sending.
objectionMap.call_later: No problem. What exact day and time works around service? I do not want to catch you during a rush again.

reviewValueProps: Use review themes about favorite dishes, hospitality, and atmosphere as proof near the menu.
Make menu, hours, address, map link, and phone visible for customers deciding at meal time.
Turn repeated review mentions of signature items into concrete menu highlights.
siteTemplateHint: Use a warm hospitality template: appetite-friendly photo hero, palette inspired by the cuisine with one accent, menu section rendered as readable text (not just an image), hours, address with a map link, and a sticky phone CTA.
customerPersonaHint: Hungry locals or visitors searching by name or cuisine on a phone, deciding in seconds whether to come in, looking for hours, the menu, and a clear address with a phone number.

marketSignals.label: Menu is missing, image-only, stale, or trapped on a third-party profile.
marketSignals.evidenceHint: Public search result lacks readable menu text or hours are inconsistent.

marketSignals.label: Customers decide during lunch or dinner windows and need fast certainty.
marketSignals.evidenceHint: Cuisine/category and review volume indicate local search demand.

marketSignals.label: Third-party delivery profiles own too much of the search journey.
marketSignals.evidenceHint: DoorDash, Yelp, or Facebook are clearer than the owned page.

leadSources: Google Maps restaurant and cuisine categories

restrictedClaims: allergen guarantees unless owner supplies policy
requiredDisclosures: menu accuracy disclaimer when owner has not approved

serviceOffer.headline: Menu, hours, and map page for a restaurant
serviceOffer.customerOutcome: Hungry customers can see the menu, hours, address, and phone fast enough to choose the restaurant.

packages.name: Menu page
packages.description: Readable menu, hours, address, map link, phone CTA, review proof, and hosting.

packages.name: Menu refresh
packages.description: Update seasonal items, hours, photos, or specials.

serviceOffer.refundPolicy: Refund before build work starts if the owner cannot approve menu or hours.

fulfillmentRequirements: allergen or alcohol copy reviewed if present
vendorRequirements: operator approval for alcohol, allergen, or health-sensitive copy
qaRules: menu is readable text or clearly approved image
hours, phone, address, and map link work
no unapproved allergen, health, or delivery guarantees
mobile first viewport shows menu path and address

portalCopy.intakePrompt: Confirm menu source, hours, address, phone, photos, and ordering or dine-in preference.

reviewStrategy: request reviews mentioning signature items and service
use real favorite-dish mentions as proof near the menu

marginModel.notes: Menu updates create strong retention if owner can provide clean menu sources.
